frameworks.name: anchoring-and-adjustment
frameworks.one_line: Inferences start from an anchor value and are adjusted toward the target.

frameworks.name: affect inference / theory of mind
frameworks.one_line: Inferring others' feelings from brief scenario descriptions.

core_question: Do people use irrelevant semantic and numerical anchors when inferring others' feelings from scenarios, and does time pressure reduce adjustment?
key_assumptions: Affect inference proceeds via an anchor-then-adjust process
Adjustment from an anchor is effortful and resource-limited
Experimenter-supplied irrelevant values can act as anchors
key_findings: Affect inferences were biased toward the supplied anchor
Bias appeared in a between-subject design across two studies
Time pressure increased anchoring under high anchors but not low anchors
interpretations: People integrate context with inference but adjust away from initial anchors
Time pressure discourages adequate adjustment, increasing anchor bias
